mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals growing consumer demand, which bolsters corporate revenues and earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return is negative more than half the time when RSAFS increases for two consecutive months, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) by more than 0.2 percentage points over a three-month period indicates weakening labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending and earnings growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day return following such an unemployment increase is positive or not statistically different from zero (t-test p>0.05), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: The combined signal of a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS below 2.5%) and strong retail sales (RSAFS above 500,000) indicates accommodative monetary policy alongside robust consumer demand, a favorable combination that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with strong demand
failure_condition: If the forward 20-day return is negative in more than 40% of the periods when both conditions hold, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During an industrial recession (when INDPRO has declined for at least three consecutive months), the economy is likely near a trough, and any subsequent stabilization or uptick in INDPRO signals recovery, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return is not significantly positive (mean > 0%) in periods where INDPRO has been declining for three months, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter increase in real GDP (GDPC1) above its long-term growth trend indicates strong economic expansion, supporting corporate earnings and equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return is negative when GDP growth is positive, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the CPI (CPIAUCSL) of more than 0.5% signals rising inflation, which erodes real earnings and increases the likelihood of monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day return following such inflation spikes is not negative and statistically significant (t-test p<0.05), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment (PAYEMS) increases for two consecutive months while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) simultaneously decreases over the same period, it signals an overheating labor market that may prompt wage inflation and tighter monetary policy, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases for 2 months and UNRATE decreases for 2 months
failure_condition: If the forward 20-day return is positive in more than 50% of the periods with joint labor market tightening, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing market recovery phase (where housing starts, HOUST, have risen from a 6-month low), further increases in housing starts signal sustained economic momentum and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return following a housing start increase from a 6-month low is not positive on average, the hypothesis is rejected.
